name: Point File In TOP
description: The Point File In TOP loads 3D point data into TOPs from either a single file or a sequence of files. Points are composed of one or more floating point values such as XYZ positions, RGB color values, 3D normals, scanner intensity, etc. The Point File in TOP will load all available point data, but only four channels can be placed into the output image. By default, the first four point data channels are placed into the red, green, blue and alpha channels respectively; however, you can assign any point channel to a colour channel using the parameters on the Point Data page. Attach a Point File Select TOP to create additional output images from the same source file.
summary: The Point File In TOP loads 3D point data into TOPs from either a single file or a sequence of files. Points are composed of one or more floating point values such as XYZ positions, RGB color values,

parameters.name: File
parameters.label: File
parameters.description: The path and name of the point file to load.

parameters.name: Reload
parameters.label: Reload
parameters.description: reload - Change from 0 to 1 to force the file to reload. Useful when the file changes or did not exist at first.

parameters.name: Reload Pulse
parameters.label: Reload Pulse
parameters.description: reloadpulse - Immediately reload the point data file.

parameters.name: Red
parameters.label: Red
parameters.description:
red -  - Select one of the available point data channels to place it into the red channel of the output image. Selecting One or Zero will place the constance value into the output channel.

 Zero - One -

parameters.name: Zero
parameters.label: Zero
parameters.description: Zero - One -

parameters.name: Green
parameters.label: Green
parameters.description:
green -  - Select one of the available point data channels to place it into the green channel of the output image. Selecting One or Zero will place the constance value into the output channel.

 Zero - One -

parameters.name: Blue
parameters.label: Blue
parameters.description:
blue -  - Select one of the available point data channels to place it into the blue channel of the output image. Selecting One or Zero will place the constance value into the output channel.

 Zero - One -

parameters.name: Alpha
parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description:
alpha -  - Select one of the available point data channels to place it into the alpha channel of the output image. Selecting One or Zero will place the constance value into the output channel.

 Zero - One -

parameters.name: Play Mode
parameters.label: Play Mode
parameters.description:
playmode -  - Specifies the method used to play the animation, there are 3 options.

 locked - This mode locks the animation to the timeline. Scrubbing or jumping in the timeline will change the animation position accordingly. The parameters Play, Reset, Speed, and Index are disabled in this mode since the timeline is directly tied to  position. specify - This mode allows the user to specify a particular position in the animation using the Index parameter below. Use this mode for random access to any location in the movie. sequential - This mode continually plays regardless of the timeline position (the Index parameter is disabled). Reset and Speed parameters below are enabled to allow some control.

parameters.name: Locked to Timeline
parameters.label: Locked to Timeline
parameters.description: locked - This mode locks the animation to the timeline. Scrubbing or jumping in the timeline will change the animation position accordingly. The parameters Play, Reset, Speed, and Index are disabled in this mode since the timeline is directly tied to  position. specify - This mode allows the user to specify a particular position in the animation using the Index parameter below. Use this mode for random access to any location in the movie. sequential - This mode continually plays regardless of the timeline position (the Index parameter is disabled). Reset and Speed parameters below are enabled to allow some control.

parameters.name: Play
parameters.label: Play
parameters.description: play - Animation plays when 1, stops when 0.

parameters.name: Speed
parameters.label: Speed
parameters.description: speed - This is a speed multiplier which only works when Play Mode is Sequential. A value of 1 is the default playback speed. A value of 2 is double speed, 0.5 is half speed and so on. Negative values will play the animation backwards.

parameters.name: Cue
parameters.label: Cue
parameters.description: cue - Jumps to Cue  when set to 1. Only available when Play Mode is Sequential.

parameters.name: Cue Pulse
parameters.label: Cue Pulse
parameters.description: cuepulse - Instantly jumps to the Cue  position in the movie.

parameters.name: Cue Point
parameters.label: Cue Point
parameters.description: cuepoint - Set any index in the animation as a point to jump to.

parameters.name: Cue Point Unit
parameters.label: Cue Point Unit
parameters.description:
cuepointunit -  - Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage).

 indices - frames - seconds - fraction -

parameters.name: I
parameters.label: I
parameters.description: indices - frames - seconds - fraction -

parameters.name: Cue Behavior
parameters.label: Cue Behavior
parameters.description:
cuebehavior -  - Customize the Cue parameter's behavior.

 repeat - When releasing the Cue parameter, immediately play the next frame. play - When releasing the Cue parameter, first play the cued frame before continuing to play the next frame.

parameters.name: On Release, Repeat Cued Frame
parameters.label: On Release, Repeat Cued Frame
parameters.description: repeat - When releasing the Cue parameter, immediately play the next frame. play - When releasing the Cue parameter, first play the cued frame before continuing to play the next frame.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.label: Index
parameters.description: index - This parameter explicitly sets the file sequence position when Play Mode is set to Specify Index. The units menu on the right lets you specify the index in the following units: Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage). For example, assume you have a sequence that internally is 25 fps, and the timeline that is 60 fps. If you set Units to Index and the parameter value to 25, you get the image that is 1 second into the sequence. If you set the Units to Frames and set the value to 60 you get the same image at 1 second into the movie.

parameters.name: Index Unit
parameters.label: Index Unit
parameters.description:
indexunit -  - Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage).

 indices - frames - seconds - fraction -

parameters.name: Loop Crossfade
parameters.label: Loop Crossfade
parameters.description: loopcrossfade - Crossfades the beginning and end of the animation together to create a smooth transition when looping. If the movie uses Trim options, it will crossfade Trim Start with Trim End positions.

parameters.name: Loop Crossfade Unit
parameters.label: Loop Crossfade Unit
parameters.description:
loopcrossfadeunit -  - Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage).

 indices - frames - seconds - fraction -

parameters.name: Step Size
parameters.label: Step Size
parameters.description: stepsize - Sets how many frames to skip before displaying next frame. For example, a StepSize of 30 will display every 30th frame. The timing of the animation playback does not change, so with a Step Size of 30 and a sample rate of 30, a new frame will be displayed every second.

parameters.name: Audio Loop
parameters.label: Audio Loop
parameters.description:
audioloop -  - This menu helps you determine how to treat the audio as the end of an animation approaches. This is needed because in some cases of playing an animation, like when driving with an index, the  will not know if you intend to loop it or not.

 silence - Audio will go silent when the animation ends. fade - Audio will fade out when the animation ends. match -

parameters.name: Silence
parameters.label: Silence
parameters.description: silence - Audio will go silent when the animation ends. fade - Audio will fade out when the animation ends. match -

parameters.name: Image Sequence Indexing
parameters.label: Image Sequence Indexing
parameters.description:
imageindexing -  - Determines how a file sequence is ordered.

 zero - Index the sequence of files starting at 0, after sorting them alphanumerically. filename - Index the sequence of files using the numbers on the end of the filenames. I.e a file named flower400.obj will be frame index 400, regardless of if there are other files in the directory before it.

parameters.name: Zero Based
parameters.label: Zero Based
parameters.description: zero - Index the sequence of files starting at 0, after sorting them alphanumerically. filename - Index the sequence of files using the numbers on the end of the filenames. I.e a file named flower400.obj will be frame index 400, regardless of if there are other files in the directory before it.

parameters.name: Interpolate Frames
parameters.label: Interpolate Frames
parameters.description: interp - Interpolates between frames based based on exact time. For example, if the index (in frames) is 1.5, then frames 1 and 2 will be blended 50-50. If the index is 1.7 then 30% of frame 1 is blended with 70% of frame 2 and so on.

parameters.name: Loading/Error Image
parameters.label: Loading/Error Image
parameters.description:
loadingerrorimage -  - When the file can not be loaded for some reason, select what to display instead.

 coloredbottomright - zero -

parameters.name: Colored Bottom Right Square
parameters.label: Colored Bottom Right Square
parameters.description: coloredbottomright - zero -

parameters.name: Trim
parameters.label: Trim
parameters.description: trim - Enables the parameters below to set trim in and out points.

parameters.name: Trim Start
parameters.label: Trim Start
parameters.description: tstart - Trim the starting point of the movie.

parameters.name: Trim Start Unit
parameters.label: Trim Start Unit
parameters.description:
tstartunit -  - Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage).

 indices - frames - seconds - fraction -

parameters.name: Trim End
parameters.label: Trim End
parameters.description: tend - Trim the ending point of the movie.

parameters.name: Trim End Unit
parameters.label: Trim End Unit
parameters.description:
tendunit -  - Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage).

 indices - frames - seconds - fraction -

parameters.name: Extend Left
parameters.label: Extend Left
parameters.description:
textendleft -  - Determines how the  File In  handles animation positions that lie before the Trim Start position. For example, if Trim Start is set to 1, and the animation's current index is -10, the Extend Left menu determines how the animation position is calculated.

 hold - Displays the first frame in the animation range (the frame specified by Trim Start) for any position before Trim Start. cycle - Loops the movie range continuously. mirror - Loops the animation range in a zig-zag pattern. For example, playing backwards from Trim Start the animation will index will climb towards Trim End, at which point is will decend down towards Trim Start again and continue to zig-zag (or mirror) the further the animation plays backwards. black - Displays black frame for any animation position before Trim Start. zero -

parameters.name: Hold
parameters.label: Hold
parameters.description: hold - Displays the first frame in the animation range (the frame specified by Trim Start) for any position before Trim Start. cycle - Loops the movie range continuously. mirror - Loops the animation range in a zig-zag pattern. For example, playing backwards from Trim Start the animation will index will climb towards Trim End, at which point is will decend down towards Trim Start again and continue to zig-zag (or mirror) the further the animation plays backwards. black - Displays black frame for any animation position before Trim Start. zero -

parameters.name: Extend Right
parameters.label: Extend Right
parameters.description:
textendright -  - Determines how the  File In  handles animation positions that lie after the Trim End position. For example, if Trim End is set to 20, and the animation's current index is 25, the Extend Right menu determines how the movie position is calculated.

 hold - Displays the last frame in the animation range (the frame specified by Trim End) for any position after Trim End. cycle - Loops the animation range continuously. mirror - Loops the animation range in a zig-zag pattern. For example, playing forwards from Trim End the movie will index will start decending towards Trim Start, at which point is will start climbing towards Trim End and continue to zig-zag (or mirror) the further the animation plays. black - Displays black frame for any animation position after Trim End. zero -

parameters.name: Override Sample Rate
parameters.label: Override Sample Rate
parameters.description: overridesample - Turn On to change the sample rate of the movie. When loading an image sequence, use these parameters to set the playback speed for the sequence.

parameters.name: Sample Rate
parameters.label: Sample Rate
parameters.description: samplerate - Set the sample rate for playback when 'Override ' above is On.

parameters.name: Pre-Read Frames
parameters.label: Pre-Read Frames
parameters.description: prereadframes - Sets how many animation frames TouchDesigner reads ahead and stores in memory. The  File In  will read and decode frames of the animation into CPU memory before they are used, this can eliminate pops or stutters in playback that occur from some frames taking too long to decode, other resources accessing the hard drive, or looping. When reading a sequence of files, having more Pre-Read Frames will allow multiple files to be decode at the same time. This allows playback of heavy file formats such as .exr in real-time, assuming the machine has enough CPU cores.

parameters.name: Frame Read Timeout
parameters.label: Frame Read Timeout
parameters.description: frametimeout - The time (in milliseconds) TouchDesigner will wait for a frame from the hard drive before giving up. If the Disk Read Timeout time is reached, that frame is simply skipped. This also works for network files that are downloaded via http://.

parameters.name: Frame Timeout Strategy
parameters.label: Frame Timeout Strategy
parameters.description:
frametimeoutstrat -  - When on, if the Disk Read Timeout is reached TouchDesigner will use the latest available frame in place of the skipped frame.

 keep - Keep showing the same frame that is currently being output. playback - Try to pick the best frame for playback. This may be a newer frame that what is shown, but not quite the frame that is requested. seeking - Try to show any frame, either before or after the target frame. Good for when seeking the movie via a shuttle widget in a UI.

parameters.name: Keep Frame
parameters.label: Keep Frame
parameters.description: keep - Keep showing the same frame that is currently being output. playback - Try to pick the best frame for playback. This may be a newer frame that what is shown, but not quite the frame that is requested. seeking - Try to show any frame, either before or after the target frame. Good for when seeking the movie via a shuttle widget in a UI.

parameters.name: Always Load Initial Frame
parameters.label: Always Load Initial Frame
parameters.description: alwaysloadinitial - If this parameter is turned on, then for the first loaded frame the Frame Read Timeout will be ignored, and it will always wait for the first frame to ensure the node always starts up with a valid image.

parameters.name: File Open Timeout
parameters.label: File Open Timeout
parameters.description: opentimeout - The time (in milliseconds) TouchDesigner will wait for a file to open. If the Disk Open Timout is reached, the  File In  will stop waiting and make its image all black, with a grey square in the bottom right corner. If the file still isn't opened the next time the  cooks, it'll wait again, and do the same. It'll keep doing this until the file is opened, or the open fails.

parameters.name: Async Upload to GPU
parameters.label: Async Upload to GPU
parameters.description: asyncupload - When enabled, this will use OpenGL features to upload movie images to the  asynchronously. This will reduce the cook time of the Movie File In  considerably (in the performance monitor the lines that say "Uploading Image to " will go down to almost nothing). There is a  memory cost to using this feature however. It uses up another (Width * Height * 4 * Read Ahead Size) bytes of  memory. If you are having poor results with this feature, make sure your graphics drivers are up to date.

parameters.name: Update Image
parameters.label: Update Image
parameters.description: updateimage - Image will not update when set to 0. Animation index will continue to move forward but the output image will not update.

parameters.name: Max Decode CPUs
parameters.label: Max Decode CPUs
parameters.description: maxdecodecpus - Limit the maximum number of CPUs that will be used to decode certain file codecs that are capable of multi-CPU decoding.

parameters.name: High Performance Read
parameters.label: High Performance Read
parameters.description: highperfread - This option should be used when playing back files that require very high SSD read speeds. It greatly improves read performance in those cases. It should not be used for low resolution or low data rate files.

parameters.name: High Performance Read Factor
parameters.label: High Performance Read Factor
parameters.description: highperfreadfactor - When doing high performance reads, this parameter controls the size of the read operations that are done on disk. Whatever the largest operation the codec asks to be done, this is multiplied by the read factor and all subsequent reads will read that much data instead. This can result in higher throughput depending on the drives. For example if a request is made to read 1MB and the factor is set to 3, then instead the operations will read 3MB from the disk and the extra 2MB read will be ready for the next frame and will likely already have the next 2 frames available in CPU RAM.

parameters.name: Hardware Decode
parameters.label: Hardware Decode
parameters.description: hwdecode - Controls if this node should use hardware decoding via the Nvidia hardware decoder chip. You can check if hardware decoding is being used using the Info , 'hardware_decode' channel. This parameter does nothing for Hap and NotchLC codecs, which are always hardware decoded.

parameters.name: Output Resolution
parameters.label: Output Resolution
parameters.description:
outputresolution -  - quickly change the resolution of the 's data.

 useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Grow or shrink the input resolution to fit this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. limit - Limit the input resolution to be not larger than this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. custom - Directly control the width and height.

parameters.name: Use Input
parameters.label: Use Input
parameters.description: useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Grow or shrink the input resolution to fit this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. limit - Limit the input resolution to be not larger than this resolution, while keeping the aspect ratio the same. custom - Directly control the width and height.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.label: Resolution
parameters.description:
resolution -  - Enabled only when the  parameter is set to Custom . Some Generators like Constant and Ramp do not use inputs and only use this field to determine their size. The drop down menu on the right provides some commonly used resolutions.

 resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: W
parameters.label: W
parameters.description: resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: Resolution Menu
parameters.label: Resolution Menu
parameters.description: resmenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used resolutions.

parameters.name: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.label: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.description: resmult - Uses the Global  Multiplier found in Edit>Preferences>TOPs. This multiplies all the TOPs resolutions by the set amount. This is handy when working on computers with different hardware specifications. If a project is designed on a desktop workstation with lots of graphics memory, a user on a laptop with only 64MB VRAM can set the Global  Multiplier to a value of half or quarter so it runs at an acceptable speed. By checking this checkbox on, this  is affected by the global multiplier.

parameters.name: Output Aspect
parameters.label: Output Aspect
parameters.description:
outputaspect -  - Sets the image aspect ratio allowing any textures to be viewed in any size. Watch for unexpected results when compositing TOPs with different aspect ratios. (You can define images with non-square pixels using xres, yres, aspectx, aspecty where xres/yres != aspectx/aspecty.)

 useinput - Uses the input's aspect ratio. resolution - Uses the aspect of the image's defined resolution (ie 512x256 would be 2:1), whereby each pixel is square. custom - Lets you explicitly define a custom aspect ratio in the Aspect parameter below.

parameters.name: Aspect
parameters.label: Aspect
parameters.description:
aspect -  - Use when Output Aspect parameter is set to Custom Aspect.

 aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect1
parameters.label: Aspect1
parameters.description: aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect Menu
parameters.label: Aspect Menu
parameters.description: armenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used aspect ratios.

parameters.name: Input Smoothness
parameters.label: Input Smoothness
parameters.description:
inputfiltertype -  - This controls pixel filtering on the input image of the .

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Nearest Pixel
parameters.label: Nearest Pixel
parameters.description: nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Fill Viewer
parameters.label: Fill Viewer
parameters.description:
fillmode -  - Determine how the  image is displayed in the viewer.
NOTE:To get an understanding of how TOPs work with images, you will want to set this to Native  as you lay down TOPs when starting out. This will let you see what is actually happening without any automatic viewer resizing.


 useinput - Uses the same Fill Viewer settings as it's input. fill - Stretches the image to fit the edges of the viewer. width - Stretches image to fit viewer horizontally. height - Stretches image to fit viewer vertically. best - Stretches or squashes image so no part of image is cropped. outside - Stretches or squashes image so image fills viewer while constraining it's proportions. This often leads to part of image getting cropped by viewer. nativeres - Displays the native resolution of the image in the viewer.

parameters.name: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.label: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.description:
filtertype -  - This controls pixel filtering in the viewers.

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. Use this to get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail. When the input is 32-bit float format, only nearest filtering will be used (regardless of what is selected).

parameters.name: Passes
parameters.label: Passes
parameters.description: npasses - Duplicates the operation of the  the specified number of times. For every pass after the first it takes the result of the previous pass and replaces the node's first input with the result of the previous pass. One exception to this is the GLSL TOP when using compute shaders, where the input will continue to be the connected 's image.

parameters.name: Channel Mask
parameters.label: Channel Mask
parameters.description: chanmask - Allows you to choose which channels (R, G, B, or A) the  will operate on. All channels are selected by default.

parameters.name: Pixel Format
parameters.label: Pixel Format
parameters.description: Pixel Format parameter.
